rationale.R1.1: Positioning requires accurately reconstructing the paper's own contribution claim and gap statement; the final paragraph's novelty assertion ('first design to deliver a credibly identified causal estimate') must be understood and validated before it can be positioned against anything.
rationale.R1.2: The three-thread argument has an implicit syllogistic structure (thread A lacks causality, thread B lacks causal ID, thread C predates LLMs → we are the unique intersection); logical consistency of that structure is central to whether the positioning holds.
rationale.R1.3: Each thread's characterization carries factual claims about what prior work does and does not do (e.g., descriptive studies 'stop at description'); those claims must align with the actual literature for the positioning to be credible.
rationale.R1.4: This is the load-bearing purpose of the delegation: establishing the precise delta between CrowdCausal and the nearest prior work in each thread and confirming no existing work already occupies the claimed design-space point.
rationale.R1.7: Coverage and attribution of prior literature directly determine whether the three threads are adequately representative; missing a key IV-in-platform-rollout paper or a causal crowdsourcing study would invalidate the 'no prior work' claim.
